hotspots.title: AfD’s poll surge and leadership ambitions
hotspots.blurb: Germany's far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) continues to dominate polls, leading in multiple states and sparking debates over its extremist ties and potential government roles. The party's radical policy proposals, including deportation forces and migration bans, have alarmed mainstream parties and civil society groups.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its potential to reshape German politics, with fears of extremist governance and erosion of democratic norms.

hotspots.title: Far-right protests and UK bans
hotspots.blurb: The UK government has banned 11 far-right figures from entering the country ahead of a major rally led by Tommy Robinson, citing threats to public order. The event has sparked counter-protests and raised tensions over immigration, free speech, and extremism.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to fears of violence, societal polarization, and the normalization of far-right extremism in mainstream discourse.

hotspots.title: Manuel Adorni corruption scandal deepens
hotspots.blurb: Argentina's Chief of Staff Manuel Adorni faces escalating corruption allegations, including unexplained wealth, luxury property purchases, and cash payments to contractors. The scandal has sparked congressional investigations and raised questions about President Milei's judgment.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to its potential to destabilize Argentina's government, undermine economic reforms, and damage Milei's anti-corruption image.

hotspots.title: Javier Milei’s coup allegations and inflation battle
hotspots.blurb: Argentinian President Javier Milei has accused political opponents of plotting a coup, while his government struggles with economic instability, soaring inflation, and mass protests over austerity measures. The crisis has raised questions about his leadership and policy direction.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to fears of political instability, economic collapse, and the erosion of democratic institutions in Argentina.

hotspots.title: Poland’s PiS party faces internal crisis
hotspots.blurb: Poland's ruling Law and Justice (PiS) party is grappling with internal divisions, leadership struggles, and declining poll numbers. The crisis has been exacerbated by controversies involving key figures like Zbigniew Ziobro and tensions over U.S. military deployments.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to its potential to reshape Polish politics, impact NATO relations, and influence the country's democratic trajectory.
